title: Deliverables written outside the workspace — archive contains no source, README, or tests
evidence: run_dir has only TASK.md/logs/meta/scores; find . -type f shows no src/*.ts. _agent_stdout.log lists files created at absolute path /Users/adriancockcroft/code/book-api/... and a file-mutation verifier refused a write to the workspace temp path. The code exists only at /Users/adriancockcroft/code/book-api (external, hardcoded).
suggestion: Agent must create files relative to the provided workspace cwd, not a hardcoded /Users/... path. As archived, the run cannot be built or run and is not reproducible.

title: Not all tests pass; agent stopped mid-task with failing validation tests
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.6917 (~31% of tests not passing). _agent_stdout.log: 'some validation tests need minor adjustments', 'A few tests are failing', ends asking 'Would you like me to continue fixing...'. .hermes_usage.json completed=false.
suggestion: Finish aligning the failing validation tests with the implementation before the run is considered complete.

title: validateBookInput middleware is defined but never wired into the router
evidence: src/validation.ts exports validateBookInput, but src/controller.ts createBookRouter registers POST /books with controller.createBook only; createBook re-implements validation inline. The middleware is dead code and duplicates logic.
suggestion: Either mount validateBookInput on POST/PUT routes or remove the module to avoid divergent validation paths.

title: Validation is stricter than the spec (year and isbn treated as required)
evidence: TASK.md/R9 requires only title and author. src/controller.ts createBook returns 400 when year<=0 or isbn is empty; src/validation.ts likewise. Valid-per-spec input lacking year/isbn is rejected.
suggestion: Confirm intended strictness; spec mandates only title+author as required.
